message:
fix(search): prevent panic on invalid string slice boundaries

The search index sometimes returns byte positions for word highlights that
land inside multi-byte UTF-8 characters (like the curly apostrophe).
When `snap_to_word_start` and `snap_to_word_end` attempted to slice the
underlying `&str` at these raw byte indices, Rust correctly panicked
to prevent invalid UTF-8 data, which resulted in a core dump of the
entire `am` CLI tool (since it runs with `panic = "abort"`).

This patch ensures all arbitrary byte positions derived from Tantivy are
rounded down to the nearest valid character boundary using
`text.floor_char_boundary(pos)` before slicing the text.
